_comment_source: r5tools.io launch thread — 12 tweets, Tuesday 8:15 PM ET post window. Char limit 280 assumed. Media paths are absolute so Typefully's file-picker resolves them regardless of import working dir. TODO media items are placeholders — swap in the real asset (see media/*.TODO) before importing.
_comment_schema: Typefully's public import format at the time of this draft accepts { thread: [{text, media[], delay_after_seconds}] }. If the current import UI diverges, this file also reads cleanly as a manual reference; each entry maps to one composer field.

thread.text:
Built 8 tools that make our alliance win every Season 2 event. Here's how they work — thread down.

Landing planner. Heat sim. Coal calc. Freeze risk. Roster extract. City cap scheduler. Season timeline. Hive grid.

Free with code RONY-FREE at r5tools.io

#LastWarSurvival

1/ Landing Planner.

Paste your roster. Pick your Assembly Point tile. Tool spits out coordinates for every member — R5+titled R4s on Ring 1, everyone else auto-placed by power. Zero 3x3 gaps (every gap is a teleport pad for enemies).

Replaces the Google Sheet.

2/ Heat Simulator.

Type your Alliance Furnace level + coordinates. Overlay your hive. Tool shows the exact tiles your radius covers and — critically — the tiles that will freeze first when the -70C Week 4 blizzard hits.

Turns "I think we're fine" into a screenshot.

3/ Coal Burn Calculator.

L20 Alliance Furnace burns 1,440 coal/min normal, 5,760/min overdrive. Every HHF in your alliance overdrives simultaneously during a severe blizzard. Your total burn is 20-40x what most R5s budget for.

Calc does the math for your roster.

4/ Freeze Risk Dashboard.

For every member: current tile temp + HHF output + AF radius coverage, then probability they freeze in the next scheduled blizzard.

Red = will freeze. Yellow = borderline. Green = safe.

R5s pin the red list in alliance chat and the reds fix it fast.

5/ Roster Extractor.

Screen-record your alliance member list on your phone (30 sec). Drop the mp4 into r5tools.io. OCR pulls every member name + power + rank into a CSV in about 2 minutes.

No account. No email. Works today.

r5tools.io

6/ City Capture Planner.

Season 2 caps you at 2 city captures per day, 36-hour post-cap protection window. Miss the window and you overlap protections and waste a slot.

Planner schedules your 6-city + 4-dig-site campaign across the season. R5 sets targets, tool sequences.

7/ Season Timeline.

One screen: every VS Day theme (Mon-Sat), every Marshal's Guard slot, every blizzard tier per week, every Rare Soil War stage transition. Overlaid with your alliance's local reset time.

Officers stop asking "when is Enemy Buster this week."

8/ Hive Grid Manager.

The one that started this suite. Multi-alliance mega-hives (up to 6 color-coded). Missile threat sim — models a Warlord Missile launch and shows which of your bases evict on impact.

Corner-stack silos, plan Ring 1 mud coverage, done.

Quick pause — I'm not a studio.

R5 of RONY in WZ 2007. Built r5tools.io because our officer team was doing landing coords in a shared Sheet for the 4th season in a row and I lost patience.

Every tool solves a problem I hit in my own alliance first.

Pricing:

Free tier — real. Landing, heat, coal, freeze, city cap, timeline, hive grid all work with no code.

$10 personal — historical roster tracking + Discord PNG exports.

$30 alliance — whole alliance forever. First 10 alliances only, then $50.

No subscription.

That's the suite. 8 tools, 7 languages (EN/KR/ES/PT/JA/DE/FR), built by an active R5.

Try it: r5tools.io — paste RONY-FREE at checkout.

Reply with which tool your alliance would use first. I'll answer every one.

#LastWarSurvival

post_thread_actions: Pin position 1 immediately after position 12 posts
From KR alliance account, quote-retweet position 1 with KR body from kr-translation-thread.md tweet 1
Reply to every reply on positions 1-12 within 4 hours of launch
At H+24, quote-retweet the highest-engagement middle tweet with a follow-up observation
